<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kaya-no-hime]][[es:Kaya-no-hime]][[fa:کایا-نو-هیمه]][[ja:カヤノヒメ]][[zh:鹿屋野比賣神]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kaya-no-hime]][[es:Kaya-no-hime]][[fa:کایا-نو-هیمه]][[ja:カヤノヒメ]][[zh:鹿屋野比賣神]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kaya-no-hime]][[es:Kaya-no-hime]][[fa:کایا-نو-هیمه]][[ja:カヤノヒメ]][[zh:鹿屋野比賣神]]


{{Short description|Shinto kami}}
{{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=カヤノヒメ|date=October 2022}}
{{Page numbers needed|date=August 2021}}
{{Infobox deity
| type         = Japanese
| name         = Kaya-no-hime
| deity_of     = Goddess of vegetation, grass and fields
| member_of    = 
| image        = 
| caption      = 
| other_names  = {{Nihongo|Kanoyanohime-no-Kami|鹿屋野比売神|}}, {{Nihongo|Kusanooyakaya-no-hime|草祖草野姫|}}, {{Nihongo|Kusano-hime|草野姫命|}}, and {{Nihongo|Nozuchi|野槌|}}
| script_name  = Japanese
| script       = 草祖草野
| parents      ={{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} and [[Izanagi]]; {{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; {{ill|Izanami|qid=Q682306|1=Izanami}}| siblings     = [[Ōyamatsumi]]
| offspring    =
| gender       =Female; {{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}| consort= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
}}

{{Nihongo|'''Kaya-no-hime'''|草祖草野|}}, also called {{Nihongo|'''Kanoyanohime-no-Kami'''|鹿屋野比売神|}}, {{Nihongo|'''Kusanooyakaya-no-hime'''|草祖草野姫|}}, {{Nihongo|'''Kusano-hime'''|草野姫命|}}, or {{Nihongo|'''Nozuchi'''|野槌|}} (meaning Moorland Elder),<ref>{{Cite web|title=Kayanohime • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kayanohime/|access-date=2020-10-14|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Nozuchi-no-kami&pg=PA501|title=Japan Encyclopedia|author=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|language=en}}</ref> is the Japanese goddess of vegetation, grass, and fields. She is considered protector of fields.<ref> {{Cite book|last1=Picken|first1=Stuart D. B.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yA3_11C6pPgC&dq=Kaya-no-hime&pg=PA99|title=Essentials of Shinto: An Analytical Guide to Principal Teachings|last2=Picken|first2=Stuart|date=1994|publisher=Greenwood Publishing Group|isbn=978-0-313-26431-3|language=en}} </ref><ref>{{Cite book|last1=Buttimer|first1=Anne|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=zUHFyGQcJxgC&q=Nozuchi-no-kami&pg=PA51|title=Nature and Identity in Cross-Cultural Perspective|last2=Wallin|first2=L.|date=1999-07-31|publisher=Springer Science & Business Media|isbn=978-0-7923-5651-6|language=en}}</ref> She is also considered the ancestor of {{ill|Herb|en|Herb|qid=Q207123|lt=Herb|1=Herb}}s.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Aveni|first=Anthony|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=ueolEAAAQBAJ&dq=Kaya-no-hime&pg=PA144|title=Creation Stories: Landscapes and the Human Imagination|date=2021-04-20|publisher=Yale University Press|isbn=978-0-300-25124-1|pages=144|language=en|author-link=Anthony Aveni}}</ref>

She is the daughter of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} and [[Izanagi]]. She is married to her husband and brother [[Ōyamatsumi]], and gave birth to eight deities. Some versions of her origin story claim she is also the mother of [[Konohanasakuya-hime]].<ref> {{Cite book|last1=Coulter|first1=Charles Russell|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=VWxekbhM1yEC&dq=Kaya-no-hime&pg=PT604|title=Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities|last2=Turner|first2=Patricia|date=2013-07-04|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-135-96397-2|language=en}} </ref>

== Worship ==
In the past, people often prayed to her before cutting down wood or reeds for building.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Hastings|first1=James|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=nZ5DAQAAMAAJ&q=Kaya-no-hime&pg=PA467|title=Encyclopaedia of Religion and Ethics: Sacrifice-Sudra|last2=Selbie|first2=John Alexander|date=1921|publisher=T. & T. Clark|isbn=978-0-567-06509-4|language=en}}</ref> She was worshipped because the plants that she favored provided the raw materials for furniture and houses.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Aston|first=W. G.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=FT6yDwAAQBAJ&dq=Kaya-no-hime&pg=PA120|title=Shinto|date=2019-09-25|publisher=BoD – Books on Demand|isbn=978-3-7340-7989-4|pages=120|language=en}}</ref>

== References ==
{{reflist}}
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

{{ill|Hime|en|Hime|qid=Q3889712|lt=Hime|1=Hime}}           

{{ill|Hime|en|Hime|qid=Q3889712|lt=Hime|1=Hime}}           {{ill|Kaya|en|Kaya|qid=Q200909|lt=Kaya|1=Kaya}}

{{ill|Hime|en|Hime|qid=Q3889712|lt=Hime|1=Hime}}           

{{ill|Hime|en|Hime|qid=Q3889712|lt=Hime|1=Hime}}

{{translated page|ja|カヤノヒメ|version=100167159|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[en:Kaya-no-hime]]
[[en:Kaya]]
[[ja:カヤノヒメ]]

<!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime--><!--Kaya-no-hime-->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
[[es:Kaya-no-hime]]
[[fa:کایا-نو-هیمه]]
[[zh:鹿屋野比賣神]]
<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
[[es:Kaya-no-hime]]
[[fa:کایا-نو-هیمه]]
[[zh:鹿屋野比賣神]]
{{wikidata link|Q8191878}}
[[Category:Goddesses]]
[[Category:Japanese deities]]
[[Category:Japanese goddesses]]
[[Category:Kunitsukami]]
[[Category:Shinto]]
[[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]
[[Category:Vegetation gods]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
